summary: Demand evidence agents completed and PRD readiness passed.

demand_status.summary: Demand is not PRD-ready; missing slots, blockers, assumptions, or evidence remain.

triage.explanation: Careful route required by hard triggers; factual or risky claims need evidence before PRD.

problem: Existing YOLO demand evidence dispatch API/schema behavior must support research/fetch style evidence without mixing external facts with target-project facts. During this scenario, use an available web/fetch/search-capable tool on https://example.com only as external background evidence.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Agents can use web/fetch/search-capable tools as external evidence, mark fetched/web records scope=external, and still require project-scoped path evidence for target-project facts.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Agents can use web/fetch/search-capable tools as external evidence, mark fetched/web records scope=external, and still require project-scoped path evidence for target-project facts.
risks: External research could be mistaken for actual project implementation facts.

blockers.message: explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
cross-checker must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
cross-checker must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.

protocol.objective: Find primary project facts for the requested demand without editing files.
protocol.prompt: Read only the relevant files, configs, logs, docs, and current implementation. Return claims with exact paths/lines or source summaries. Separate assumptions from facts.

evidence_tasks.reason: Factual project claims need primary evidence.

protocol.objective: Independently verify high-risk factual claims and challenge the explorer result.
protocol.prompt: Use independent reads or sources. Confirm, weaken, or contradict each claim. Any conflict must become a blocker instead of being averaged away.
protocol.required_when: evidence_policy === cross_check or claim risk is high

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

evidence_tasks.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

state.next_action: Run explorer + cross-checker + verifier before treating project claims as facts.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch already implements complete support for research/fetch style external evidence with proper scope=external marking and project-fact boundary enforcement.

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES defines four scope values: project, external, user, unknown
evidence.why: Core data model supports external scope declaration

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeErrors validates that all evidence records declare scope as project, external, user, or unknown
evidence.why: Schema enforcement ensures scope is always declared

evidence.summary: demandRequestsExternalResearch detects explicit external research requests via URLs or fetch/search keywords
evidence.why: System can detect when external evidence is required

evidence.summary: externalEvidencePresent checks if agent results include scope=external evidence with URL or external source
evidence.why: Validation verifies external evidence was actually gathered

evidence.summary: Agent prompt instructs: 'If the demand explicitly asks for external research/fetch/search, actually use an available web/fetch/search-capable tool. Record those records as scope=external'
evidence.why: Agent protocol explicitly requires external tool usage and scope marking

evidence.summary: Post-execution validation adds EXTERNAL_RESEARCH_EVIDENCE_REQUIRED blocker when external research was requested but no scope=external evidence found
evidence.why: Runtime enforcement prevents missing external evidence

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION requires path for project scope, url for external scope, and mandatory scope field
evidence.why: Schema enforces proper evidence structure per scope type

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues adds PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE blocker when evidence is only external/user/unknown for existing-project facts
evidence.why: Prevents external evidence from being used as project facts

evidence.summary: Doctrine states: 'Project facts require project-scoped evidence from code, tests, docs, config, logs, or artifacts, with a repo-relative path or file locator'
evidence.why: Product requirements explicitly separate project vs external evidence

evidence.summary: Agent prompt allows web/fetch/search tools with scope=external marking when agentToolProfile includes 'research'
evidence.why: Tool access control supports external research mode

evidence.summary: Successfully fetched https://example.com using mcp__web_reader__webReader tool during this evidence gathering
evidence.why: Live verification that external fetch tools are functional and can be used by agents

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot creates pre-execution snapshot of all project files outside allowed artifact roots
evidence.why: Boundary enforcement detects any unauthorized project mutations

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after snapshots to detect any added, modified, or deleted project files
evidence.why: Post-execution audit identifies boundary violations

evidence.summary: Post-execution boundary diff generates BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers for any project files changed outside allowed artifact root
evidence.why: Runtime enforcement prevents target project mutations

risks: If claim is wrong, agents might use external evidence as project facts or mutate project files during evidence gathering

result.notes: The YOLO demand evidence dispatch system fully implements the acceptance criteria: (1) web/fetch/search tools are available and functional, (2) agent protocol explicitly instructs to mark fetched records as scope=external, (3) schema validation enforces scope declaration, (4) PRD readiness validation prevents external evidence from satisfying project-fact requirements, (5) boundary audit prevents target project mutations. The demand is already satisfied by existing implementation.

evidence.summary: executionConfig normalizes agent_tool_profile to 'research' for research/fetch/web tool profiles
evidence.why: Tool access control supports external research mode

evidence.summary: Agent prompt allows web/fetch/search tools with scope=external marking when agentToolProfile includes 'research'
evidence.why: Tool access control supports external research mode

assumptions: The explorer used mcp__web_reader__webReader to fetch https://example.com during evidence gathering, but this is not present in the code artifacts I reviewed
risks: If claim is wrong, agents might use external evidence as project facts or mutate project files during evidence gathering

result.notes: Independent verification confirms the explorer's claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch fully implements external evidence support with (1) four-scope data model including 'external', (2) agent protocol requiring scope=external marking for fetched/web evidence, (3) schema validation enforcing scope declarations, (4) PRD readiness validation preventing external evidence from satisfying project-fact requirements, (5) boundary audit preventing target project mutations. All evidence verified from actual source files with corrected line numbers. The system correctly separates project-scoped facts from external research evidence.

agent_results.claim: Explorer and cross-checker evidence is substantially verified, but one external evidence claim remains unverified and should be treated as an assumption rather than a fact.

evidence.summary: Both agents verified the complete implementation of external evidence support including VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES, validation functions, boundary snapshots, and agent protocol instructions
evidence.why: Core implementation evidence is consistent across both independent verifications

evidence.summary: Schema enforcement and project-fact boundary validation confirmed by both agents with corrected line numbers
evidence.why: Cross-checker provided line number corrections, confirming independent verification

evidence.summary: Product doctrine explicitly separates project vs external evidence requirements
evidence.why: Documentation evidence supports implementation claims

evidence.summary: Explorer claims to have fetched https://example.com using mcp__web_reader__webReader tool, but cross-checker could not verify this from code artifacts
evidence.why: This represents the only unverifiable claim in the evidence chain

assumptions: The explorer successfully used mcp__web_reader__webReader to fetch https://example.com during evidence gathering, as this could not be independently verified by the cross-checker from available artifacts
Web/fetch/search-capable tools are available to agents during evidence dispatch execution, as this depends on runtime environment configuration not visible in static code review
risks: If external evidence tools are not actually available at runtime, agents cannot fulfill the protocol requirement to fetch and mark external evidence as scope=external
If the assumption is wrong and no external fetch occurred, the protocol validation that checks for scope=external evidence when external research is requested would fail in production

result.notes: Verification complete: (1) All project-scoped evidence is verified with usable paths and sources by both agents independently. (2) Line number corrections from cross-checker confirm actual code review occurred. (3) The single unverifiable claim (external web fetch) is properly captured as an assumption by the cross-checker, not promoted to fact. (4) The system implementation is correctly verified. (5) Assumptions are explicitly documented rather than hidden. The evidence is sufficient to proceed with the understanding that external tool availability is a runtime dependency verified as an assumption.
